nodes.title: Robust Frequency and Timing Synchronization for OFDM

metadata.evidence_sentence: The problem of timing synchronization was first proposed in the seminal work of Schmidl and Cox [2], who introduced a correlation-based method using repeated pilot symbols.
evidence_sentences: The problem of timing synchronization was first proposed in the seminal work of Schmidl and Cox [2], who introduced a correlation-based method using repeated pilot symbols.
[3] later proposed a maximum-likelihood estimator for joint timing and frequency offset recovery, extending the pioneering work of [2] to cyclic-prefix-based detection.
The foundational work of Schmidl and Cox [2] remains a standard reference, and subsequent extensions addressed fractional offset estimation [3] and robustness under frequency-selective fading [7].
metadata.reason: Section 'introduction' and sentence keyword 'first proposed' indicate a foundational history citation.
